18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		Once         bool
28		PollInterval time.Duration
29		NoReview     bool
30		LabelFilter  string
31		SessionID    string
32		WorkerID     string
33		ProjectRoot  string
34	}
35	
36	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
37	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
38	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
39	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
40	
41	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
42		BeadID      string `json:"bead_id"`
43		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
44		WorkerID    string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
45		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
46		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
47		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
48		Status      string `json:"status"`
49		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
50		SessionID   string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
51		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
52		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
53		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
54		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
55		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
56		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
57		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
58		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
59		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
60		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
61		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
62		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
63		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
64		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
65		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
66		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
67		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
68		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
69		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
70		// compute wasted/effective spend.
71		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
72		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
73		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
74		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
75		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
76		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
77		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
78		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
79		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
80		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
81	}
82	
83	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
84		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
85	}
86	
87	type ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
88	
89	func (f ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
90		return f(ctx, beadID)
91	}
92	
93	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
94	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
95	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
96	// the current attempt.
97	//
98	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
99	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
100	// leaves the bead open.
101	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
102		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
103	}
104	
105	// SatisfactionCheckerFunc is a functional adapter for SatisfactionChecker.
106	type SatisfactionCheckerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
107	
108	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
109		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
110	}
111	
112	type ExecuteBeadLoopStore interface {
113		ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error)
114		Claim(id, assignee string) error
115		Unclaim(id string) error
116		Heartbeat(id string) error
117		CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error
118		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
119		Events(id string) ([]bead.BeadEvent, error)
120		SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail string) error
121		IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error)
122		// Reopen sets a closed bead back to open, appending notes to the bead's
123		// Notes field and recording a reopen event. Used by the post-merge review
124		// step when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK.
125		Reopen(id, reason, notes string) error
126	}
127	
128	// readyDiagnoser is the optional interface the work loop uses to explain an
129	// empty execution queue. bead.Store satisfies it via ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
130	type readyDiagnoser interface {
131		ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
132	}
133	
134	// NoReadyWorkBreakdown explains why the execution-ready queue is empty when
135	// dependency-ready beads exist. Populated on an ExecuteBeadLoopResult when
136	// NoReadyWork fires and the store exposes ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
137	type NoReadyWorkBreakdown struct {
138		SkippedEpics       []string `json:"skipped_epics,omitempty"`
139		SkippedOnCooldown  []string `json:"skipped_on_cooldown,omitempty"`
140		SkippedNotEligible []string `json:"skipped_not_eligible,omitempty"`
141		SkippedSuperseded  []string `json:"skipped_superseded,omitempty"`
142		NextRetryAfter     string   `json:"next_retry_after,omitempty"`
143	}
144	
145	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
146	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets
147	// the agent package emit events without importing the server package.
148	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
149	// these directly from the channel.
150	//
151	// Terminal phases: done, preserved, failed.
152	type ProgressEvent struct {
153		EventID   string    `json:"event_id"`
154		AttemptID string    `json:"attempt_id"`
155		WorkerID  string    `json:"worker_id"`
156		ProjectID string    `json:"project_id"`
157		BeadID    string    `json:"bead_id"`
158		Harness   string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
159		Model     string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
160		Profile   string    `json:"profile,omitempty"`
161		Phase     string    `json:"phase"`
162		PhaseSeq  int       `json:"phase_seq"`
163		Heartbeat bool      `json:"heartbeat"`
164		TS        time.Time `json:"ts"`
165		ElapsedMS int64     `json:"elapsed_ms"`
166		Message   string    `json:"message,omitempty"`
167	}
168	
169	type ExecuteBeadLoopResult struct {
170		Attempts          int                  `json:"attempts"`
171		Successes         int                  `json:"successes"`
172		Failures          int                  `json:"failures"`
173		NoReadyWork       bool                 `json:"no_ready_work,omitempty"`
174		NoReadyWorkDetail NoReadyWorkBreakdown `json:"no_ready_work_detail,omitempty"`
175		LastSuccessAt     time.Time            `json:"last_success_at,omitempty"`
176		LastFailureStatus string               `json:"last_failure_status,omitempty"`
177		Results           []ExecuteBeadReport  `json:"results,omitempty"`
178	}
179	
180	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
181	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
182	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
183	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
184		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
185		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
186		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
187		Now                 func() time.Time
188		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
189		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
190		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
191		Reviewer BeadReviewer
192	}
193	
194	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to runtime.ProgressCh non-blocking.
195	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
196	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
197		if ch == nil {
198			return
199		}
200		select {
201		case ch <- evt:
202		default:
203		}
204	}
205	
206	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
207	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, harness, model, profile, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64) ProgressEvent {
208		return ProgressEvent{
209			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
210			AttemptID: attemptID,
211			WorkerID:  workerID,
212			ProjectID: projectID,
213			BeadID:    beadID,
214			Harness:   harness,
215			Model:     model,
216			Profile:   profile,
217			Phase:     phase,
218			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
219			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
220			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
221			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
222		}
223	}
224	
225	func randomProgressID() string {
226		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
227	}
228	
229	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
230		if w.Store == nil {
231			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
232		}
233		if w.Executor == nil {
234			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
235		}
236	
237		now := w.Now
238		if now == nil {
239			now = time.Now
240		}
241		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
242		if assignee == "" {
243			assignee = "ddx"
244		}
245		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
246		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
247			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
248		}
249		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
250		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
251			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
252		}
253		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
254		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
255			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
256		}
257		harness := rcfg.Harness()
258		model := rcfg.Model()
259		profile := rcfg.Profile()
260	
261		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
262		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
263	
264		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
265			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
266		}
267	
268		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
269			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
270			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
271			"harness":      harness,
272			"model":        model,
273			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
274			"assignee":     assignee,
275			"once":         runtime.Once,
276		})
277		defer func() {
278			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
279				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
280				"successes":           result.Successes,
281				"failures":            result.Failures,
282				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
283			})
284		}()
285	
286		for {
287			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
288			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
289			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
290			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
291			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
292				return result, err
293			}
294	
295			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
296			if err != nil {
297				return result, err
298			}
299			if !ok {
300				if result.Attempts == 0 {
301					result.NoReadyWork = true
302					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
303						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
304							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
305								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
306								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
307								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
308								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
309								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
310							}
311						}
312					}
313				}
314				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
315					return result, nil
316				}
317				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
318					return result, err
319				}
320				continue
321			}
322	
323			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
324	
325			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
326			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
327			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
328			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
329				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
330					if runtime.Log != nil {
331						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
332					}
333					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
334						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
335						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
336					})
337					continue
338				}
339			}
340	
341			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
342				continue
343			}
344	
345			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
346				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
347				"title":    candidate.Title,
348				"assignee": assignee,
349			})
350	
351			if runtime.Log != nil {
352				if candidate.Title != "" {
353					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
354				} else {
355					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
356				}
357			}
358	
359			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
360			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
361			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
362			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
363			runStart := now()
364			phaseSeq := 0
365			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
366				phaseSeq++
367				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
368					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
369					harness, model, profile,
370					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
371				))
372			}
373	
374			nextPhase("queueing", false)
375	
376			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
377			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
378			hbWG.Add(1)
379			go func(beadID string) {
380				defer hbWG.Done()
381				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
382				defer ticker.Stop()
383				for {
384					select {
385					case <-hbCtx.Done():
386						return
387					case <-ticker.C:
388						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
389					}
390				}
391			}(candidate.ID)
392	
393			nextPhase("running", false)
394	
395			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
396			hbCancel()
397			hbWG.Wait()
398			if err != nil {
399				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
400					BeadID: candidate.ID,
401					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
402					Detail: err.Error(),
403				}
404			}
405			if report.BeadID == "" {
406				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
407			}
408			if report.Status == "" {
409				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
410			}
411			if report.Detail == "" {
412				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
413			}
414	
415			result.Attempts++
416	
417			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
418				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
419				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
420				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
421				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
422				reviewApproved := true
423				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || runtime.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
424	
425				if reviewSkipped {
426					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
427						return result, err
428					}
429				}
430	
431				if w.Reviewer != nil && !runtime.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
432					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
433					if reviewErr != nil {
434						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
435						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
436						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
437						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
438						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
439						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
440						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
441						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
442						attemptCount := prior + 1
443						maxRetries := rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
444						if maxRetries <= 0 {
445							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
446						}
447						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
448							InputBytes:  0,
449							OutputBytes: 0,
450						}
451						if reviewRes != nil {
452							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
453								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
454								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
455								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
456								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
457								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
458							}
459						}
460						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
461							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
462							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
463								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
464								Summary:   class,
465								Body:      body,
466								Actor:     assignee,
467								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
468								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
469							})
470							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
471							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
472							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
473							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
474							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
475							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
476						} else {
477							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
478							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
479								Kind:      "review-error",
480								Summary:   class,
481								Body:      body,
482								Actor:     assignee,
483								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
484								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
485							})
486						}
487					} else {
488						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
489						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
490						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
491						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
492						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
493						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
494						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
495						// log if the artifact write failed.
496						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
497						if artifactErr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
498							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
499						}
500	
501						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
502							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
503							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
504							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
505							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
506							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
507						}
508						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
509						case VerdictApprove:
510							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
511							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
512							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
513							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
514								Kind:      "review",
515								Summary:   "APPROVE",
516								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
517								Actor:     assignee,
518								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
519								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
520							})
521							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
522								return result, cerr
523							}
524						case VerdictRequestChanges:
525							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
526							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
527							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
528							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
529							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
530								Kind:      "review",
531								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
532								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
533								Actor:     assignee,
534								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
535								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
536							})
537							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
538							if reopenNotes == "" {
539								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
540							}
541							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
542								return result, reopenErr
543							}
544							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
545							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
546							reviewApproved = false
547						case VerdictBlock:
548							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
549							if rationale == "" {
550								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
551									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
552									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
553									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
554									Actor:     assignee,
555									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
556									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
557								})
558								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
559								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
560								report.ReviewRationale = ""
561								reviewApproved = false
562								break
563							}
564							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
565							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
566							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
567								Kind:      "review",
568								Summary:   "BLOCK",
569								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
570								Actor:     assignee,
571								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
572								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
573							})
574							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
575							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
576								return result, reopenErr
577							}
578							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
579							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
580							reviewApproved = false
581						}
582					}
583				}
584	
585				if reviewApproved {
586					result.Successes++
587					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
588				} else {
589					result.Failures++
590					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
591				}
592			} else {
593				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
594					return result, err
595				}
596				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
597					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
598					if cerr != nil {
599						return result, cerr
600					}
601					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale)
602					if aerr != nil {
603						return result, aerr
604					}
605					if satisfied {
606						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
607						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
608						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
609						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
610						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
611						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
612						// satisfied beads).
613						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
614						if evidence != "" {
615							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
616							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
617							report.Detail = evidence
618						}
619						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
620						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
621							return result, cerr
622						}
623						result.Successes++
624						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
625					} else {
626						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
627						// move on to other beads.
628						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
629							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
630							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
631								return result, cerr
632							}
633							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
634						}
635						result.Failures++
636						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
637					}
638				} else {
639					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
640						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
641						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
642							return result, err
643						}
644						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
645					}
646					result.Failures++
647					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
648				}
649			}
650	
651			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
652	
653			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
654			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
655			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
656			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
657			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
658				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
659					return result, err
660				}
661			}
662	
663			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
664			terminalPhase := "failed"
665			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
666				terminalPhase = "done"
667			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
668				terminalPhase = "preserved"
669			}
670			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
671			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
672			if finalAttemptID == "" {
673				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
674			}
675			phaseSeq++
676			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
677				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
678				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
679				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
680				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
681				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
682				Harness:   harness,
683				Model:     model,
684				Profile:   profile,
685				Phase:     terminalPhase,
686				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
687				Heartbeat: false,
688				TS:        now().UTC(),
689				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
690				Message:   report.Detail,
691			})
692	
693			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
694				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
695				"status":               report.Status,
696				"detail":               report.Detail,
697				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
698				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
699				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
700				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
701				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
702				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
703			})
704	
705			if runtime.Log != nil {
706				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
707			}
708	
709			if runtime.Once {
710				return result, nil
711			}
712		}
713	}

715	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
716		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
717		if err != nil {
718			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
719		}
720		for _, candidate := range ready {
721			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
722				continue
723			}
724			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
725				continue
726			}
727			return candidate, true, nil
728		}
729		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
730	}

784		}

786			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))

788		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
789			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
790		}
791		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
792			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
793		}
794		if report.ResultRev != "" {
795			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
796		}
797		if report.BaseRev != "" {
798			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
799		}
800		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
801			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))

804		return bead.BeadEvent{
805			Kind:      "execute-bead",
806			Summary:   report.Status,
807			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
808			Actor:     actor,

814	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
815	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
816	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
817	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
818	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
819	// never break the core execute-loop.
820	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
821		if sink == nil {
822			return
823		}
824		if data == nil {
825			data = map[string]any{}
826		}
827		entry := map[string]any{
828			"session_id": sessionID,
829			"type":       eventType,
830			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
831			"data":       data,
832		}
833		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
834		if err != nil {
835			return
836		}
837		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
838		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
839	}

841	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
842	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
843	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
844		switch report.Status {
845		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
846			shortRev := report.ResultRev
847			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
848				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
849			}
850			if shortRev != "" {
851				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
852			}
853			return "merged"
854		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
855			return "already_satisfied"
856		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
857			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
858				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
859			}
860			return "no_changes"
861		default:
862			detail := report.Detail
863			if detail == "" {
864				detail = report.Status
865			}
866			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
867				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
868			}
869			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
870		}
871	}

873	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
874	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
875	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
876	
877	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
878	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
879	
880	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
881	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
882	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
883	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
884	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
885		if rationale == "" {
886			return false
887		}
888		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
889	}

891	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
892	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
893	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
894	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
895	//
896	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
897	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
898	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
899	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
900	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale string) (bool, string, error) {
901		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
902			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
903		}
904		if rationaleIsSpecific(rationale) {
905			evidence := rationale
906			return true, evidence, nil
907		}
908		if noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose {
909			return true, fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount), nil
910		}
911		return false, "", nil
912	}

914	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
915		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
916			return false
917		}
918		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev
919	}

921	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
922	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
923	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
924	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
925	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
926	//     embed the class in their message).
927	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
928	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
929	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
930	//     matches.
931	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
932		if reviewRes != nil {
933			switch reviewRes.Error {
934			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
935				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
936				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
937				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
938				return reviewRes.Error
939			}
940		}
941		msg := ""
942		if reviewErr != nil {
943			msg = reviewErr.Error()
944		}
945		switch {
946		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
947			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
948		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
949			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
950		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
951			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
952		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
953			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
954		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
955			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
956		}
957		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
958	}
959	
960	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
961	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
962	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
963	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
964	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
965	
966	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
967	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
968	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
969	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
970	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
971		if resultRev == "" {
972			return 0
973		}
974		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
975		if err != nil {
976			return 0
977		}
978		n := 0
979		for _, ev := range events {
980			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
981				continue
982			}
983			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
984			if m == nil {
985				continue
986			}
987			if m[1] == resultRev {
988				n++
989			}
990		}
991		return n
992	}
993	
994	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
995	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
996	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
997	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
998	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
999	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1000		var b strings.Builder
1001		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1002		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1003		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1004		if message != "" {
1005			b.WriteString("\n")
1006			b.WriteString(message)
1007		}
1008		return b.String()
1009	}

1011	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
1012		if d <= 0 {
1013			return nil
1014		}
1015		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
1016		defer timer.Stop()
1017		select {
1018		case <-ctx.Done():
1019			return ctx.Err()
1020		case <-timer.C:
1021			return nil
1022		}
1023	}

16	// TestRunWithConfigDelegation verifies that ExecuteBeadWorker.RunWithConfig
17	// resolves a *Config built via config.NewTestConfigForLoop and threads every
18	// loop-relevant durable knob through to the running loop's observable
19	// behavior. SD-024 Stage 1 / Bead 6.
20	func TestRunWithConfigDelegation(t *testing.T) {
21		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
22	
23		opts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
24			Assignee:                "rwc-worker",
25			ReviewMaxRetries:        7,
26			NoProgressCooldown:      42 * time.Minute,
27			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: 5,
28			HeartbeatInterval:       2 * time.Second,
29			Harness:                 "rwc-harness",
30			Model:                   "rwc-model",
31		}
32		cfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(opts)
33		rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(opts))
34	
35		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
36			Store: store,
37			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
38				return ExecuteBeadReport{
39					BeadID:    beadID,
40					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
41					Detail:    "merged cleanly",
42					SessionID: "sess-rwc",
43					ResultRev: "rwcdead",
44					Harness:   "rwc-harness",
45					Model:     "rwc-model",
46				}, nil
47			}),
48		}
49	
50		var sink bytes.Buffer
51		runtime := ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
52			Once:      true,
53			EventSink: &sink,
54			SessionID: "sess-rt",
55			WorkerID:  "wkr-rt",
56		}
57	
58		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, runtime)
59		require.NoError(t, err)
60		require.NotNil(t, result)
61		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
62		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
63	
64		// Decode loop.start event from the EventSink. The loop emits structured
65		// JSONL there; the harness, model, and assignee fields prove the durable
66		// rcfg values flowed through RunWithConfig into the running loop.
67		var startData map[string]any
68		for _, line := range bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace(sink.Bytes()), []byte("\n")) {
69			var entry map[string]any
70			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(line, &entry))
71			if entry["type"] == "loop.start" {
72				startData, _ = entry["data"].(map[string]any)
73				break
74			}
75		}
76		require.NotNil(t, startData, "loop.start event missing from EventSink")
77		assert.Equal(t, "rwc-harness", startData["harness"])
78		assert.Equal(t, "rwc-model", startData["model"])
79		assert.Equal(t, "rwc-worker", startData["assignee"])
80		assert.Equal(t, "wkr-rt", startData["worker_id"])
81		assert.Equal(t, "sess-rt", startData["session_id"])
82		assert.Equal(t, true, startData["once"])
83	
84		// Bead-level evidence: claim used the configured assignee, and the
85		// late execute-bead event records that assignee as Actor.
86		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
87		require.NoError(t, err)
88		require.NotEmpty(t, events)
89		var sawExecuteBead bool
90		for _, ev := range events {
91			if ev.Kind == "execute-bead" {
92				assert.Equal(t, "rwc-worker", ev.Actor, "execute-bead event Actor must be rcfg assignee")
93				sawExecuteBead = true
94			}
95			if ev.Kind == "routing" {
96				assert.True(t, strings.Contains(ev.Body, "rwc-harness"),
97					"routing event body must reference rcfg harness; body=%s", ev.Body)
98			}
99		}
100		assert.True(t, sawExecuteBead, "expected an execute-bead event recording the configured assignee")
101	}
